commit.message:
feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles (#181)

* Initial plan

* feat: automate Cloudflare DNS record creation in Ansible roles

Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(dns_record): add input validation, error handling, and zone ID caching

- Assert required variables upfront for clear failure messages
- Add failed_when checking Cloudflare API success field to catch
  logical failures returned with HTTP 200
- Add changed_when for accurate idempotency reporting
- Use verbosity-gated no_log so errors are visible with -vvv
- Cache zone ID across multiple role inclusions in same play
- Add meta/main.yml matching project conventions

* docs: update DNS documentation for automatic provisioning

DNS A records are now auto-provisioned during app deployment via the
dns_record role. Update first-deployment guide, managing-records,
batch-operations, cloudflare-setup, infrastructure-layers, ansible-roles
guide, and troubleshooting docs to reflect this change.

* feat(cockpit): add dns_record integration with Tailscale IP

Cockpit binds to the Tailscale interface, so its DNS A record must
point to the Tailscale IP rather than the public VPS IP.

* fix(dns_record): validate Cloudflare success flag on all API calls

- Add failed_when on zone lookup and record lookup GET requests
- Check for exactly 1 zone result instead of just non-empty
- Use verbosity-gated no_log on assert task

* fix(dns_record): domain-scoped cache, IPv4 validation, drift detection

- Cache zone IDs in a dict keyed by domain (safe for multi-domain plays)
- Validate dns_record_ip matches IPv4 format in assert block
- Fail explicitly when multiple A records found for same FQDN
- Update task now triggers on IP, TTL, or proxied changes (not just IP)

* docs: add cockpit to integrated apps lists, fix troubleshooting causes

- Include cockpit in all dns_record integration app lists
- Broaden batch-operations example to include all non-integrated apps
- Rewrite troubleshooting causes to reflect realistic failure scenarios

* fix(dns_record): strict IPv4 octet-range validation, update docs example

- Tighten regex to reject out-of-range octets (e.g. 999.999.999.999)
- Update managing-records example to show only non-integrated apps

* fix(dns_record): prevent token leakage, use idiomatic regex test

- Keep no_log: true on all URI tasks to prevent Bearer token exposure
  in CI logs or shared terminals at any verbosity level
- Surface Cloudflare API errors via separate fail tasks that log only
  sanitized json.errors field
- Switch is regex() to is match() for Ansible idiom consistency

* fix(dns_record): include HTTP status and msg fallback in error messages

All fail tasks now surface status code and Ansible's msg field when
Cloudflare json.errors is unavailable (timeouts, TLS errors, non-JSON
responses).

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <38692952+sripwoud@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: sripwoud <me@sripwoud.xyz>

commit.message:
layout: implement Panel.SetAlign cross-axis alignment

Add Align type and constants to internal/ui/pctwidget, wire SetAlign/Align
through the public Panel API, and update AutoLayout to apply cross-axis
positioning (AlignStart/Center/End/Stretch) for both Row and Column containers.

Also fixes a latent bug: layoutChildren now uses the panel's own FlowDirection
and Align when recursing into children, rather than inheriting the parent's
flow direction from the outer AutoLayout call.

Resolves AUDIT.md HIGH finding: 'Panel.SetAlign is a documented no-op'.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(render): integrate Intel EU and AMD RDNA shader backends into rendering path

Phase 4.3 — Intel EU / AMD RDNA shader pipeline integration (AUDIT.md item).

## Changes

### Rust (render-sys)
- Add render-sys/src/submit.rs: bind_eu_shader_to_batch and
  bind_rdna_shader_to_batch compile WGSL to native EU/RDNA machine code,
  allocate GEM buffers, emit SolidColorPipeline state with relocation entries,
  and return the shader GPU address.  gpu_gen_to_intel / gpu_gen_to_rdna
  conversion helpers with unit tests.
- Add render_submit_shader_batch FFI in lib.rs: auto-detects GPU from DRM
  device, delegates to the Intel (i915/Xe) or AMD (AMDGPU) path.

### Go (internal/render)
- binding.go: add SubmitShaderBatch(drmPath, wgslSrc, isFragment, contextID)
  CGO wrapper + ShaderSubmitError type.

### Go (internal/render/backend)
- gpu.go: embed solid_fill.wgsl via go:embed, add solidFillShader field,
  compileSolidFillShader helper called at New() time.
- submit.go: submitToGPU now calls maybeSubmitShaderBatch (best-effort, non-
  fatal) before the fixed-function batch submission.  Extracted to a helper to
  keep cyclomatic complexity at baseline levels.
- solid_fill.wgsl: copy from render-sys/shaders/ for go:embed availability.

### Tooling
- cmd/gpu-shader-demo/main.go: new demo binary; detects GPU, compiles vertex
  and fragment shaders, creates context, submits shader batch.
- Makefile: add gpu-shader-demo target (same static-link pattern as others).
- API.md: document GPU Rendering Pipeline section with data flow, Go API, GPU
  generation table.

Closes AUDIT.md: "Intel EU and AMD RDNA shader backends not integrated"

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
infra: code quality and infrastructure improvements

Logging:
- Add app/core/logging_setup.py: centralized RotatingFileHandler (10MB/5 backups),
  StreamHandler, standard format, KOTO_LOG_LEVEL env var override
- Call setup_logging() from src/server.py at startup
- Replace ~90 print() calls with logger in 4 files:
  smart_dispatcher.py (28), training_db.py (28), training_data_builder.py (21),
  local_model_router.py (10) — mapped to logger.info/error by context

CI/CD:
- Raise coverage threshold 20% -> 40%
- Add --cov-report=xml:coverage.xml + upload coverage artifact (14-day retention)
- Add Bandit security scan step (medium+ severity) to Lint & Format job

Requirements:
- Remove duplicate PyPDF2 from requirements.txt (pypdf>=4.0.0 is the successor)
- Fix requirements_voice.txt: google-generativeai -> google-genai>=1.0.0

Docker:
- Add non-root user (useradd koto, chown, USER koto) for security hardening
- Increase HEALTHCHECK start-period 10s -> 30s (gunicorn startup time)

Code quality:
- Fix bare except Exception at agent_routes.py:1661 (add logger.debug)
- Add app/core/config_defaults.py with DEFAULT_MODEL constant
- Update agent_routes.py and unified_agent.py to use DEFAULT_MODEL

commit.message:
fix(csp): allow blob: worker-src for FA2 layout Web Worker

The ForceAtlas2 layout supervisor creates a Web Worker from a blob: URL.
The previous CSP had no worker-src directive, causing it to fall back to
script-src which blocked blob: origins. This silently disabled the
force-directed layout in production, leaving nodes in a static ring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language (#667)

* fix: prevent grouping consecutive code blocks with the same language

Two consecutive code blocks with the same language (e.g., both `mjs`)
were incorrectly grouped into a CJS/MJS switchable tab. Add a check
to ensure the two code blocks have different languages before creating
a switchable code tab.

Refs: https://github.com/nodejs/node/pull/62181

refactor: consolidate top clone groups, reduce duplication 3.20%→2.89%

- wire/args.go: new ArgDecoder (sequential decoder, no per-arg if-err boilerplate)
  and ParseArgXxx helpers (Uint32, Int32, Int, Bytes, String, MinLen)
- input/keyboard.go, pointer.go, touch.go: refactor 20 event handlers to use
  wire.ArgDecoder, collapsing 4 lines per arg into 1 line per arg
- output/protocol.go: replace private geomDecoder/getInt32Arg/getStringArg with
  public wire.ArgDecoder; update handleMode to use ArgDecoder
- vertex.go: extract makeUniformQuad helper; refactor rectToVertices,
  roundedRectToVertices, boxShadowToVertices to use it
- commands.go: extract emitShaderKernelState; reduce State3DPS/State3DVS to 1-line bodies
- bezier.go: extract pointToLineSegDistSq; refactor isQuadraticFlat/isCubicFlat
- cmd/*-demo: convert 5 demo mains to demo.RunDemoWithSetup, remove boilerplate

Result: clone pairs 56→50, duplicated lines 960→865, ratio 3.20%→2.89%
All tests pass (go test -race ./...)

commit.message:
fix: force 24h time format for Nordic locales (#110)

* fix: force 24h time format for Nordic locales

- Use effectiveLocale instead of locale in useFormatters() so Auto mode
  resolves to a concrete locale string (e.g. sv-SE) instead of undefined
- Add hourCycle: h23 override for Nordic locales to prevent browser/OS
  12h format overrides even when the locale conventionally uses 24h
- Remove unused date-fns from Vite manualChunks (caused empty chunk
  build error)

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Fix: auto-calculate points and update weekly score when completing tasks via MCP

Port calculatePoints() and getMonday() helpers into MCP server.
When tasks_update transitions a task to done, auto-calculate points
(matching the renderer's scoring logic) and increment the weekly score.

Previously, tasks completed via CLI/MCP bypassed the score update
that only lived in InboxView.tsx, causing stale weekly totals.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add terminal chart rendering for Kusto render queries (#22)

* Surface Kusto render metadata

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Render Kusto charts in the terminal

Add opt-in `query --chart` support that renders compatible Kusto render metadata as Hex1b terminal charts in human output and Mermaid charts in markdown output. The CLI now surfaces compatibility hints and unsupported messages, rejects `--format json --chart`, and includes tests and docs for the new behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix chart rendering review issues

Address the substantive issues found in review by rejecting sparse grouped charts instead of fabricating zeroes, honoring stacked line layouts for human rendering, treating case-distinct grouped values separately, waiting for stable Hex1b output, and sanitizing Mermaid control characters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Azure.Identity auth chain with PAT support and actionable errors (#19)

* feat: Azure.Identity auth chain with PAT support and actionable errors

Add a richer credential chain for AzDO authentication:
  AZDO_TOKEN (auto-detect PAT vs Bearer) → AzureCliCredential → az CLI → anonymous

Changes:
- New AzdoCredential record carries token + auth scheme + source label
- PAT auto-detection: non-JWT tokens use Basic auth automatically
- Azure.Identity AzureCliCredential step (targeted, not DefaultAzureCredential)
- Session-level caching of credential source (fail once, skip for session)
- Actionable 401/403 errors: name the org, show what was tried, give fix steps
- Updated CI guide: devdiv repos (macios/android) now say 'auth required' not 'tools don't work'
- README: new Authentication section documenting the credential chain
- Tests: PAT detection, scheme selection, error messages, fallback order

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(graph): remove circular layout override to preserve semantic node positioning

Node positions are computed by orderPatchGraphNodesForReveal (relationship-aware
ring layout). Applying circularLayout was destroying semantic positioning and
causing all nodes to appear in a flat ring.

Also adds extended test coverage for graph build and layout logic.

Fix falsy enum values returning None instead of name

AlarmReason.NO_ALARM has value 0, making it falsy in Python. The
_enum_to_lowercase function used 'if enum_value' which incorrectly
returned None for NO_ALARM. Changed to 'if enum_value is not None'
to properly handle zero-valued Flag enums.

This caused battery_monitor alarm and dc_energy_meter alarm to show
as 'unknown' instead of 'no_alarm' in Home Assistant.

commit.message:
Add VO2max estimate trend to run stats

Computes per-run VO2max estimate (ml/kg/min) using:
  VO2 = 0.2 * speed_m_per_min + 3.5  (ACSM flat-ground running equation)
  VO2max ≈ VO2 / (avg_hr / hr_max)

HR_max is derived from the maximum max_hr recorded across all runs.
Runs with < 15 min, < 2 km or avg HR < 100 bpm are excluded (unreliable).

- New VO2max trend chart (teal) in run stats screen
- 'Best VO2max' chip in summary row
- 'Best VO2max est.' entry in personal records (tappable → run detail)
- Pure Flutter computation, no backend changes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

backend: implement GPU text rendering via SDF glyph atlas

textToVertices was a no-op (returned nil) pending Phase 5.2.
Implement it by:
- Adding FontAtlas *text.Atlas to Config and GPUBackend struct
- Threading the atlas through packBatchVertices and commandToVertices
- Generating textured quad vertices (6 per glyph) with UV coords
  addressing each glyph's region in the SDF atlas texture
- Scaling glyph positions by fontSize/atlas.Baseline
- Graceful nil return when atlas is absent or string is empty

Also wire AutoConfig.Atlas into GPU Config so the atlas set by
callers of NewRenderer is available in the GPU path, not only in
the software fallback.

Adds four unit tests covering nil atlas, empty string, per-glyph
vertex/color/UV correctness, and horizontal advance ordering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
consumer: implement GPU→CPU readback in copyToBuffer via DMA-BUF mmap

Replace the no-op placeholder with a real implementation that:
- Calls Present() to obtain the render target's DMA-BUF file descriptor
- Validates the fd (fd < 0 → error)
- mmaps the fd read-only with MAP_SHARED to access ARGB8888 pixels
- Bulk-copies pixel rows into the caller-supplied primitives.Buffer,
  resizing buf.Pixels and updating Width/Height/Stride as needed
- Defers fd close and munmap for correct cleanup on all paths

Cyclomatic complexity: 6 (well within the ≤10 threshold).

Tests added:
- TestGPUConsumerRenderWithBufferInvalidFd: buf != nil + fd=-1 → error
- TestGPUConsumerCopyToBuffer: file-backed fdMockGPURenderer, verifies
  pixel data is faithfully copied into the target buffer

Fixes AUDIT.md HIGH finding: "GPU→CPU readback is unimplemented (no-op)".

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(render): generate cgo_flags_generated.go so go test ./... works after go generate

scripts/build-rust.sh now writes internal/render/cgo_flags_generated.go at the
end of the build with a #cgo LDFLAGS directive pointing at the just-built
librender_sys.a and dl_find_object_stub.o using CGO's ${SRCDIR} interpolation.

After running `go generate ./...`, contributors can run:
  CC=musl-gcc CGO_ENABLED=1 go test ./...
without manually setting CGO_LDFLAGS.

The generated file is added to .gitignore (it is arch-specific build output).
generate.go doc comment, README, and CONTRIBUTING are updated to reflect the
new workflow. AUDIT.md CRITICAL item marked resolved.

Closes: AUDIT.md CRITICAL - go test ./... fails without CGO_LDFLAGS

commit.message:
Show directory picker when creating customizations with multiple source folders (#301250)

* feat: auto-select single source folder in prompt selection

* Show directory picker when creating customizations with multiple source folders

When the Sessions app creates a new customization (agent, skill,
instructions, prompt), the target directory was always auto-resolved
to the first default location (e.g., .github/agents). This ignored
that the system may be reading from multiple directories (e.g.,
.github/agents and .claude/agents).

Now, if multiple source folders exist for the target storage type,
a quick pick is shown to let the user choose. If only one folder
exists, it is used directly without prompting.

This applies to both the manual creation flow (createNewItemManual)
and the AI-guided creation flow (createWithAI) in the management
editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: update accessibilitySupport and fontInfo types to any, and placeholder type to string | undefined

* Fix regression: pass undefined targetDir through to command

When no matching folder exists for the requested storage type (e.g.
skills have no user-storage folder), the previous code silently
aborted. The original behavior passed undefined through, letting
the command show its own folder picker via askForPromptSourceFolder.

Use null to signal user cancellation (dismiss picker) vs undefined
to signal no folder available. Also address review feedback:
- Use folder directly when length=1 in customizationCreatorService
- Use generic localized placeholder string
- Revert unrelated askForPromptSourceFolder.ts change

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Filter workspace picker to project root children only

getSourceFolders() tags tilde-expanded user paths (e.g. ~/.claude/agents)
as PromptsStorage.local, causing them to appear in the workspace picker.
Filter local folders to only those under the active project root so only
actual workspace directories are shown.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add 'New AGENTS.md' option to Instructions dropdown in Sessions

When in the Instructions section of the Sessions app, the + button
dropdown now includes a 'New AGENTS.md' option that creates the file
at the workspace root. Uses the AGENT_MD_FILENAME constant from
promptFileLocations.ts — no hardcoded filenames.

If the file already exists, it opens it in the embedded editor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: prevent unfiltered folder picker when no matching storage exists

When the user clicks 'New Skill (User)' but no user-storage folder
exists for skills, the fallback returned undefined which was passed
through to the command. The command's askForPromptSourceFolder then
showed ALL folders including workspace-scoped ones.

Now when both the storage filter and the legacy fallback return
nothing, we return null (cancellation) instead of undefined
(pass-through), preventing the unfiltered picker from appearing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: use project root as boundary instead of unreliable storage tags

The storage tags from getSourceFolders() are unreliable — tilde-expanded
user paths like ~/.copilot/skills get tagged PromptsStorage.local by
getConfigBasedSourceFolders(). This caused the user picker to show
workspace folders and vice versa.

Instead, partition folders by whether they're under the active project
root: children of project root are workspace, everything else is user.
This correctly handles all customization types including skills.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix duplicate user dirs: prefer ~/.copilot as creation target

getSourceFolders() returns both ~/.claude/agents and ~/.copilot/agents
for user targets. The Sessions app's canonical creation target is
~/.copilot (per AgenticPromptsService design). Use the workspace
service's first includedUserFileRoot (~/.copilot) to filter user
folders for creation, and deduplicate by URI to remove duplicates
from config-based and override sources.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Show all user targets, just deduplicate by URI

Don't filter out user directories — show everything we read from.
The only issue was duplicates (e.g. ~/.copilot/agents appearing twice
from config-based and override sources), which the URI dedup handles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review feedback: dedup, telemetry, empty folder handling

- customizationCreatorService: deduplicate workspace folders by URI
  before length checks to avoid unnecessary picker with duplicates
- managementEditor: normalize workspace-root to workspace in telemetry
  to avoid breaking the documented schema
- managementEditor: return undefined (not null) when no matching folders
  exist, so the command can fall back to askForPromptSourceFolder

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix: update accessibilitySupport and fontInfo types to any, and placeholder type to string | undefined

* Remove monaco.d.ts from branch diff

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

poller: fix parser O(N) deletions, decouple MQTT publish, use selectors

- MQTT: daemon thread draining a SimpleQueue; main recv loop never blocks on publish

commit.message:
Fix Slice shape inference crashes with SIGABRT on empty dimensions (#7738)

Fix #7735 

This pull request addresses issues with the ONNX Slice operator when
handling empty dimensions, ensuring correct and safe shape inference in
these edge cases. The main focus is to prevent undefined behavior and
ensure that slicing on empty dimensions consistently produces outputs
with zero length, regardless of the step direction. Additional
regression tests are included to verify these fixes, and several test
data files are updated to reflect the new behavior.

**Slice operator improvements for empty dimensions:**

* Updated the `processSliceInputs` function in `defs.cc` to
short-circuit and produce a zero-length output when the input dimension
size is zero, avoiding invalid clamp bounds and potential undefined
behavior.
* Modified shape inference logic in `old.cc` to detect empty dimensions
and set the output dimension value to zero, both for standard and
expanded shape inference code paths.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR2635-R2645)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-5e200991755406044e2dbb2449683bb5c6d77583d1459f0065a756813dd1b59bR6329-R6335)

**Testing and regression coverage:**

* Added two new tests in `shape_inference_test.py` to verify that
slicing on empty dimensions with both positive and negative steps
produces the correct output shape, preventing regression of issue #7735.

**Test data updates:**

* Updated several `output_0.pb` binary test data files under
`onnx/backend/test/data/node/` (including attention, gelu,
hammingwindow, and lppool tests) to align with the corrected Slice
operator behavior on empty dimensions.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-875055c843db41ea7d579c8bd29201e6f60bb4175ac96f840bdd27d488866198L1-R7)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d6fe5631baa8b6cb320c03143f797abe3dce4ddf5363fa9cfe71cc12bc31ac53L2-R3)
[[3]](diffhunk://#diff-3275d5e3517a0e95967de3c46a75b02104980f2566f1a0cbefa386ea8e02f312L1-R3)
[[4]](diffhunk://#diff-537fe44987ef880d522c9c88283070745874abc990951f49eff8dccc35630af1L2-R2)
[[5]](diffhunk://#diff-3d57e2f0cf63b32349f6619a2f61e90d4a4aa4fdac9a794af6bfb00c97fe9f8eL1-R2)

These changes collectively improve the robustness of ONNX shape
inference and operator correctness when handling tensors with empty
dimensions.

---------

Signed-off-by: Ti-Tai Wang <titaiwang@microsoft.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle SSH key stored as single line with literal \n

The SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret has newlines stored as literal \n characters,
resulting in a 1-line key file. Added sed to convert them back.

docs: Add comprehensive goal-achievement and roadmap documentation

Adds ROADMAP.md documenting:
- Project goal-achievement summary (14/16 goals fully achieved, 2 partial, 1 deferred)
- Detailed metrics assessment (code quality, complexity, test coverage)
- Five-priority roadmap for addressing gaps:
  1. Complete GPU-rendered frame path integration (critical)
  2. Stabilize and expose public API surface
  3. Reduce demo boilerplate duplication
  4. Lower cyclomatic complexity of gen-atlas utility
  5. AT-SPI2 accessibility (deferred, requires external D-Bus runtime)
- Appendix with complex function baseline and code quality details

commit.message:
feat(presets): Pluggable preset system with catalog, resolver, and skills propagation (#1787)

* Initial plan

* feat(templates): add pluggable template system with packs, catalog, resolver, and CLI commands

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(templates): add comprehensive unit tests for template pack system

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(presets): pluggable preset system with template/command overrides, catalog, and resolver

- Rename 'template packs' to 'presets' to avoid naming collision with core templates
- PresetManifest, PresetRegistry, PresetManager, PresetCatalog, PresetResolver in presets.py
- Extract CommandRegistrar to agents.py as shared infrastructure
- CLI: specify preset list/add/remove/search/resolve/info
- CLI: specify preset catalog list/add/remove
- --preset option on specify init
- Priority-based preset stacking (--priority, lower = higher precedence)
- Command overrides registered into all detected agent directories (17+ agents)
- Extension command safety: skip registration if target extension not installed
- Multi-catalog support: env var, project config, user config, built-in defaults
- resolve_template() / Resolve-Template in bash/PowerShell scripts
- Self-test preset: overrides all 6 core templates + 1 command
- Scaffold with 4 examples: core/extension template and command overrides
- Preset catalog (catalog.json, catalog.community.json)
- Documentation: README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, PUBLISHING.md
- 110 preset tests, 253 total tests passing

* feat(presets): propagate command overrides to skills via init-options

- Add save_init_options() / load_init_options() helpers that persist
  CLI flags from 'specify init' to .specify/init-options.json
- PresetManager._register_skills() overwrites SKILL.md files when
  --ai-skills was used during init and corresponding skill dirs exist
- PresetManager._unregister_skills() restores core template content
  on preset removal
- registered_skills stored in preset registry metadata
- 8 new tests covering skill override, skip conditions, and restore

* fix: address PR check failures (ruff F541, CodeQL URL substring)

- Remove extraneous f-prefix from two f-strings without placeholders
- Replace substring URL check in test with startswith/endswith assertions
  to satisfy CodeQL incomplete URL substring sanitization rule

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments

- Move save_init_options() before preset install so skills propagation
  works during 'specify init --preset --ai-skills'
- Clean up downloaded ZIP after successful preset install during init
- Validate --from URL scheme (require HTTPS, HTTP only for localhost)
- Expose unregister_commands() on extensions.py CommandRegistrar wrapper
  instead of reaching into private _registrar field
- Use _get_merged_packs() for search() and get_pack_info() so all
  active catalogs are searched, not just the highest-priority one
- Fix fetch_catalog() cache to verify cached URL matches current URL
- Fix PresetResolver: script resolution uses .sh extension, consistent
  file extensions throughout resolve(), and resolve_with_source()
  delegates to resolve() to honor template_type parameter
- Fix bash common.sh: fall through to directory scan when python3
  returns empty preset list
- Fix PowerShell Resolve-Template: filter out dot-folders and sort
  extensions deterministically

* fix: narrow empty except blocks and add explanatory comments

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments (round 2)

- Fix init --preset error masking: distinguish "not found" from real errors
- Fix bash resolve_template: skip hidden dirs in extensions (match Python/PS)
- Fix temp dir leaks in tests: use temp_dir fixture instead of mkdtemp
- Fix self-test catalog entry: add note that it's local-only (no download_url)
- Fix Windows path issue in resolve_with_source: use Path.relative_to()
- Fix skill restore path: use project's .specify/templates/commands/ not source tree
- Add encoding="utf-8" to all file read/write in agents.py
- Update test to set up core command templates for skill restoration

* fix: remove self-test from catalog.json (local-only preset)

* fix: address Copilot PR review comments (round 3)

- Fix PS Resolve-Template fallback to skip dot-prefixed dirs (.cache)
- Rename _catalog to _catalog_name for consistency with extension system
- Enforce install_allowed policy in CLI preset add and download_pack()
- Fix shell injection: pass registry path via env var instead of string interpolation

* fix: correct PresetError docstring from template to preset

* Removed CHANGELOG requirement

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

* Applying review recommendations

---------

Co-authored-by: mnriem <15701806+mnriem@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: SSH deployment via SCP + add help button to game

- Fix deploy.yml: replace broken git checkout (--exclude not valid) with SCP
- Fix deploy-api.yml: use SCP so sed-injected credentials actually deploy
- Fix SSH key handling: use printf instead of echo to preserve key format

- Help overlay shows compact game instructions, closable to continue playing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Prevent duplicate Slack notifications when both `opened` and `review_requested` events are received for the same PR (#48)

* Initial plan

* fix: prevent duplicate Slack messages for review_requested events

When a PR is opened, OctoSlack posts a Slack message. If a
review_requested event is subsequently received for the same PR,
OctoSlack now checks whether a message already exists for that PR URL
before posting. If one is found, the duplicate notification is skipped.
If the Slack API check fails, the message is posted anyway (fail-safe).

Co-authored-by: vibe-chung <237340876+vibe-chung@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: vibe-chung <237340876+vibe-chung@users.noreply.github.com>
